title: Echocardiogram result review
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports clinical history and examination, NT-proBNP, echocardiography, ECG, chest X-ray, renal, thyroid, liver, lipid, glucose, blood count, and urinalysis review.
examination.rationale: The NICE heart-failure section supports documenting the echocardiogram source and date, reason for review, symptom and examination context, and clinician review of cardiac structure or function when heart failure is suspected; it does not create an interpretation or diagnosis.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports clinical history and examination, NT-proBNP, echocardiography, ECG, chest X-ray, renal, thyroid, liver, lipid, glucose, blood count, and urinalysis review.

source.title: Chronic heart failure in adults: diagnosis and management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.12 — history, examination, natriuretic peptides, imaging, ECG, and laboratory tests

referral.text: Supports regular primary-care review, record updates, medicine review, functional assessment, deterioration review, follow-up planning, and specialist access.
referral.rationale: The review section supports documenting clinician explanation, an entered plan, safety-net discussion, and arranged follow-up without generating management.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports regular primary-care review, record updates, medicine review, functional assessment, deterioration review, follow-up planning, and specialist access.

source.title: Chronic heart failure in adults: diagnosis and management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.4–1.1.9 — primary-care review, record, care plan, and access

follow_up.text: Supports murmur nature, family and medical history, symptoms, peripheral signs, abnormal ECG, and clinician-led echocardiography review.
follow_up.rationale: The NICE valve section confirms echocardiography relevance for clinician-assessed murmur and valve-disease context; it is conditional and does not classify a valve lesion.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports murmur nature, family and medical history, symptoms, peripheral signs, abnormal ECG, and clinician-led echocardiography review.

source.title: Heart valve disease presenting in adults: investigation and management

source_limitations: Indication, date, modality, image quality, chambers, function, valves, pressures, effusion, measurements, comparison, formal interpretation, diagnosis, severity, urgency, treatment, referral, communication, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
